records.journeyTitle: Signal To Advisor Approval Without Client Visibility
records.speakerThesis: An internal signal can move through analyst and advisor review without becoming client-visible advice.
records.captionShort: Advisor review starts, but the client still cannot see advice.
records.captionLong: The signal is reviewed internally and can be routed to an advisor; advisor approval is deliberately not a client release.
presenterNotes: Start by saying this is the core safety promise.
Point out that the advisor can act without accidentally publishing to the client.
Bridge into J02: compliance is the release authority.
records.screenPurpose: Signal queue, workbench trigger detail and advisor approval detail.
records.userIntent: Triage a potentially advice-relevant signal and route it safely.
records.trustProof: Demo action path records request-data, route-to-advisor, approval and escalation audit events.
records.complianceProof: Client visibility remains blocked after advisor approval.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not say advisor approval publishes advice.
records.caveat: This is demo-scoped workflow behavior and not production authorization.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Repository-backed UI reload of the advisor/compliance handoff state.

records.speakerThesis: Compliance, not advisor approval, controls client visibility.
records.captionShort: Compliance checks evidence before client release.
records.captionLong: The release path only succeeds when advisor approval, compliance status, evidence and permission pass the workflow gate.
presenterNotes: Show the block/request-evidence path before release.
Use the phrase: advisor approval is necessary, not sufficient.
Point to the evidence and audit proof after release.
records.screenPurpose: Compliance queue, review detail, block state, release confirmation and audit log.
records.userIntent: Release, block or request evidence for advice-like content.
records.trustProof: J02 writes compliance request/block/release evidence items and audit rows.
records.complianceProof: workflowGate checks advisor approval, compliance release, evidence status and permission.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not claim production compliance certification or real advice.
records.caveat: Demo mode uses deterministic role/tenant rules and no real auth.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Production identity, object-level grants and repository-backed compliance screens.

records.speakerThesis: The client only acts on released content, and the decision updates the evidence package.
records.captionShort: Released decision, client action, evidence proof.
records.captionLong: The principal can accept, defer, reject or request more information after the content is released to the client.
presenterNotes: Make clear this is a decision workflow demo, not final advice.
Show evidence immediately after the decision.
Mention review schedule and audit trail as the operational close.
records.screenPurpose: Decision list, digital decision room, success state and evidence record.
records.userIntent: Make or defer a client decision with proof.
records.trustProof: Decision actions update decision, participant, recommendation and evidence records.
records.complianceProof: Every decision/evidence action checks the released, client-visible recommendation context first.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not call the decision content final financial, legal or tax advice.
records.caveat: Some decision screen copy remains static demo content after mutation.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Decision room should read updated Prisma state after action.

records.speakerThesis: Client documents become controlled evidence inputs, not automatic advice.
records.captionLong: The document path records file metadata and extraction state, then queues analyst verification before evidence release.
presenterNotes: Mention that binary upload and malware scanning are intentionally not claimed.
Show extraction as draft and human-reviewed.
Connect this to evidence completeness in J02.
records.screenPurpose: Document list, upload, extraction review and verification pending states.
records.userIntent: Provide source material for review and evidence.
records.trustProof: Upload action updates Document, DocumentVersion, DocumentExtraction and evidence link metadata.
records.complianceProof: Extraction remains client-hidden and analyst review is required.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not claim real binary storage, scanning or signed download.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Object storage, malware scanning and repository-backed document UI.

records.journeyTitle: Entity Wealth Map And Action Board
records.speakerThesis: Complex wealth structures are useful only when gaps and evidence requirements remain visible.
records.captionShort: Wealth action remains blocked until evidence is complete.
records.captionLong: The entity and action path shows a restricted structure, legal review and a ready-state block when evidence is missing.
presenterNotes: Avoid positioning the wealth map as advice.
Use it as context and data-quality proof.
Connect blocked action to the evidence-by-default rule.
records.screenPurpose: Entity creation, wealth map drawer and action board gate.
records.userIntent: Inspect structure and resolve missing evidence before readiness.
records.trustProof: J05 persists entity, participant, action gate and evidence/audit records.
records.complianceProof: Sensitive jurisdiction and missing evidence block readiness.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not present the wealth map as financial advice.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Reusable evidence completeness service and DB-backed wealth/action screens.

records.speakerThesis: A tenant must be prepared with team, policy and invitation controls before the client workspace is trusted.
records.captionShort: Tenant setup creates the controlled client context.
records.captionLong: The onboarding path moves a tenant through setup, team assignment, policy and invitation proof without real authentication.
presenterNotes: Use this if the audience asks how the demo data is initialized.
Do not start the first pitch here unless the buyer is ops-heavy.
Call out that no real invitation email is sent.
records.screenPurpose: Tenant list, creation wizard, team, policies, users and invite state.
records.userIntent: Prepare a client tenant and invite the principal.
records.trustProof: J06 persists tenant setup data, team owners, policy and invite/consent records.
records.complianceProof: Tenant activation depends on readiness gates and owners.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not claim real auth, MFA or email delivery.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Real auth, invitation delivery and DB-backed tenant screens.

records.speakerThesis: Sensitive access is scoped, confirmed and auditable.
records.captionShort: Access changes require scope, confirmation and audit.
records.captionLong: The governance flow invites a user, changes a role, approves scoped access and keeps audit export controlled.
presenterNotes: Use this as the security appendix after the main trust-chain demo.
Point out second confirmation and scoped document grants.
Mention deterministic demo permissions, not production security.
records.screenPurpose: Governance users, roles, access requests and audit history.
records.userIntent: Grant scoped access without broad leakage.
records.trustProof: J07 persists invited user, role, second confirmation, access request, scoped grant, evidence and audit.
records.complianceProof: Second confirmation and audit export controls are preserved.
records.currentRealityLabel: E5 persisted demo transaction plus demo role-aware permission proof
records.overclaimRisk: Do not claim production identity or object-level policy engine completeness.
records.caveat: Object grants are simplified deterministic demo rules.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Real identity claims and object-level grant evaluator.

records.journeyTitle: Export With Scope Redaction Approval And Download
records.speakerThesis: Evidence becomes portable only after scope, redaction, approval, expiry and audit.
records.captionShort: Export is scoped, redacted, approved and audited.
records.captionLong: The export path records selected objects, excludes restricted content, applies redaction and writes package/download/share proof.
presenterNotes: This is the best closing scene.
Say metadata-only package clearly.
Point to expiry and watermark as trust signals.
records.screenPurpose: Export scope, redaction, preview, approval and download/share state.
records.userIntent: Create a safe evidence package for external or client use.
records.trustProof: J08 persists export request, generated package metadata, document version, evidence and audit proof.
records.complianceProof: Approval, redaction profile and export gate are required.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not claim real ZIP generation, object storage or signed URL.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Binary package generation, storage adapter, signed download endpoint and malware scan.

records.speakerThesis: Family context is captured as reviewable data, not transformed directly into advice.
records.captionShort: Client context becomes auditable review input.
records.captionLong: The profile, family and relationship steps update demo records and create evidence without releasing advice-like content.
presenterNotes: Use this as a human opening if the audience is client-experience oriented.
Keep the advice boundary explicit.
Mention relationship conflict/data-quality follow-up.
records.screenPurpose: Portal, profile form, family member management and relationship map.
records.trustProof: J09 persists profile, family member and relationship records with evidence/audit proof.
records.complianceProof: Actions keep clientVisible false in API response and do not release advice.
records.overclaimRisk: Do not imply automated advice from profile data.
records.caveat: Relationship validation is fixture-scoped and UI may remain static.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Reusable relationship validation and DB-backed profile/family screens.

records.speakerThesis: Platform policies explain why release, evidence, export and access gates exist.
records.captionLong: The platform screens show advice boundary, role, security, evidence and export settings that frame the workflow.
presenterNotes: Use this as a brief context frame, not as the main demo.
Point to the no-unapproved-advice policy.
Avoid claiming specific persisted policy changes.
records.screenPurpose: Platform settings, advice boundary, roles, security, evidence templates and export templates.
records.userIntent: Understand the policy and control baseline.
records.trustProof: Routes, visual states and screencast clicks demonstrate the control surface.
records.complianceProof: Policy copy and role/security states reinforce release controls.
records.currentRealityLabel: E3-E4 policy/control surface with generic demo action proof
records.overclaimRisk: Do not claim full persisted policy transaction or production security.
records.caveat: Use as frame or appendix, not primary proof.
records.nextImplementationNeed: Persisted policy/security mutations and DB-backed admin read models.
